data.title: Novartis upgraded at Morgan Stanley on stock pullback, Rhapsido launch
data.description:
• Morgan Stanley has upgraded Novartis (NVS) to overweight from equal-weight, citing a valuation pullback after the Swiss drugmaker missed on the bottom line in its Q3 results in late October as well as optimism over the upcoming launch of Rhapsido (remibrutinib) for chronic spontaneous urticaria (hives).
  • The bank has upped its price target to CHF 110 from CHF 108 (~2% upside based on Wednesday's close on the SIX Swiss Exchange.
  • The Q3 miss "was driven by a negative one-off on Cosentyx and faster-than-expected erosion for legacy/LOE products (Tasigna, Promacta, Taf/Mek, Xolair), while key growth drivers (Kesimpta, Pluvicto, Scemblix, Fabhalta) beat expectations," wrote analyst Thibault Boutherin.
  • He expects modest 2026 sales growth of 2% followed by 5% from 2027-2029, adding he sees the company projecting a 5% CAGR from 2025-2030.
  • Boutherin wrote that he anticipates $5B in risk adjusted potential sales for Rhapsido assuming additional indications, adding that the launch in chronic spontaneous urticaria is likely to surpass the consensus estimate.

data.title: Aegon downgraded to Neutral at UBS on limited upside potential to valuation
data.description:
European insurer Aegon's (AEG) shares were downgraded at UBS to Neutral from Buy, considering a limited upside potential to Sum-of-the-Parts, or SOTP, valuation.

Shares were -1.57% during late morning trading to $7.82.

UBS sees a marginal risk to the consensus 2027 free cash flow and DPS at the company's December 10 capital markets day.

UBS expects a group-wide detailed SOTP valuation range of Euro 7.0 per share to Euro 9.8 per share.

The investment bank raised its 12-month price target to Euro 7.3 from Euro 7.2, driven by a higher valuation of the ASR stake and the international business.

UBS's rating differs from the average sell-side analysts and Seeking Alpha authors rating of Buy. Quant Rating system grades the stock as Strong Buy.

data.title: Ivanhoe cuts 2026 Kamoa-Kakula copper outlook following flood
data.description:
Ivanhoe Mines (OTCQX:IVPAF) said Wednesday it has lowered guidance for FY 2026 copper production from its Kamoa-Kakula complex in the Democratic Republic of Congo to 380,000-420,000 tons, well below its initial target of 600,000 tons, as it continues to recover from flooding in the Kakula mine.

While Kamoa-Kakula has become one of the world's biggest sources of copper, Ivanhoe (OTCQX:IVPAF) has spent the past six months evaluating and reversing the impact of seismic activity in the area, and in june slashed the project's target production to 370,000-420,000 tons, down from 520,000-580,000 tons.

For 2027, Ivanhoe (OTCQX:IVPAF) said it anticipates production of 500,000-540,000 tons from Kamoa-Kakula.

The mine's "turnaround" is "advancing with confidence," Ivanhoe's (OTCQX:IVPAF) Co-chair Robert Friedland said, adding that copper production of ~400,000 tons/year during "recovery years" is an "extraordinary testament to the quality of Kamoa-Kakula's world-leading natural endowment."

Kamoa-Kakula’s long term outlook remains promising, BMO Capital analysts said recently, as the "vast majority" of the value of the underground mine which experienced the flooding "remains intact... Overall, we are not expecting a material valuation change or decrease in reserves as the changes will be spread over a very long 40-plus year mine life."

data.title: CSW Industrials rises as J.P. Morgan upgrades shares to Overweight
data.description:
CSW Industrials (CSW) rose as much as 6.5% on Wednesday after analysts at J.P. Morgan upgraded the stock to Overweight from Meutral. They pointed to improving fundamentals, attractive valuation and stronger momentum heading into fiscal 2027.

In a research note, analyst Tomohiko Sano said the firm now sees “increased confidence” in CSW’s (CSW) long-term strategy and its ability to generate above-market returns. Sano highlighted that organic growth appears to have found a floor, with the bank expecting a recovery to take hold in FY27.

J.P. Morgan said that broader industry trends remain constructive and that CSW Industrials (CSW) has shown consistent execution despite a softer demand environment. Recent acquisitions, combined with disciplined use of capital, have helped strengthen the company’s positioning across its end markets, according to the note.

The bank lifted its price target to $350, which represents roughly 26% upside from the stock’s previous close. J.P. Morgan argued that at current levels, the shares offer an appealing entry point for investors anticipating a cyclical recovery and sustained operational improvement.

data.title: Freshworks gains amid conference cancellation
data.description:
• Freshworks (FRSH) rose 2.8% amid a cancellation from a conference. 
  • Freshworks (FRSH) cancelled its appearance at the the UBS Global Technology and AI conference on Wednesday. 
  • The cancellation was due to a family matter and an illness, a Freshworks spokesperson told Seeking Alpha in an email. The executives would have been there if they could travel.

data.title: BlackRock’s Bitcoin holdings have risen more than 250% since SEC approval
data.description:
BlackRock’s (BLK) Bitcoin holdings have surged to a staggering 255.47% of their 2024 levels since SEC approval.

The world’s largest asset manager has increased its Bitcoin (BTC-USD) reserves from 303,935 BTC in the second quarter of 2024 to 776,474.65 BTC as of Dec. 1, positioning itself as the dominant institutional player in the cryptocurrency market, according to a report by BestBrokers.

“BlackRock’s steady accumulation of Bitcoin (BTC-USD) demonstrates how the market increasingly views the cryptocurrency as a hedge against inflation, tariffs, and broader economic turbulence,” said Alan Goldberg, analyst at BestBrokers.

The total assets under management across all Bitcoin ETFs have grown by 53.83% since Q2 2024, reaching 1,309,895.47 BTC by the fourth quarter of 2025.

This collective holding now represents approximately 6.72% of the total Bitcoin supply and exceeds the estimated 1,100,000 BTC owned by Bitcoin’s creator, Satoshi Nakamoto.

The 11 SEC-approved spot Bitcoin ETFs have rapidly established themselves as major market forces in less than two years of operation.

 

 

 

BlackRock’s iShares Bitcoin Trust (IBIT) has demonstrated dominance in the market, accounting for approximately 96% of the net increase in Bitcoin holdings across all ETFs in Q4 2024.

 

 

 

While most ETFs experienced outflows in recent months, BlackRock (BLK) has continued its acquisition strategy, adding a net 24,411 BTC in the current quarter despite temporary outflows of around 23,226 BTC between Nov. 1 and Dec. 1.

The firm’s strategic positioning has proven especially notable during periods of market volatility, according to the BestBrokers team. Even during Q1 2025, when nearly all other funds reported decreasing Bitcoin holdings, BlackRock increased its reserves by 23,588 BTC, more than compensating for the broader market retreat.

data.title: Glencore to cut 1,000 jobs in restructuring to focus on copper production
data.description:
Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) said Wednesday it has cut ~1,000 jobs as part of a restructuring that focuses on growing demand for copper, and merges its nickel and zinc operations; the company has ~150,000 employees worldwide.

Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) said it plans to spend heavily to boost copper output to ~1.6 million metric tons/year by 2035, after producing 950,000 tons a year ago, as part of its goal to become one of the world's biggest copper miners.

The company said it will restart its Alumbrera copper mine in Argentina in Q4 2026, aiming for production to begin in H1 2028; once fully operational, the mine is expected to produce ~75,000 tons of copper, 317,000 oz of gold and 1,000 tons of molybdenum over the four years of its operations.

Glencore (OTCPK:GLCNF) (OTCPK:GLNCY) set a capital spending budget of ~$6.5 billion annually through to 2028, although this could rise by $1 billion in 2026, $1.7 billion in 2027 and $1.5 billion in 2028 if it proceeds with additional copper growth projects.

For 2025, the company said copper production has been uneven and likely will come in at the lower end of its guidance range of 850,000-910,000 tons.

London copper has gained ~30% YTD and currently trades at a record high, spiking above $11,400/ton on Wednesday.

data.title: Bessent calls for change in how regional Fed presidents are chosen
data.description:
U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent on Wednesday called for a change in how regional Federal Reserve president are selected, amid his broader effort to reshape leadership at the monetary authority.

The 12 Fed district bank chiefs "were meant to be from their district,” Bessent said in a discussion at the New York Times DealBook Summit. Now, “there’s this idea of importing a bright, shiny object.”

He said he will "start advocating going forward, not retroactively, that regional Fed presidents must have lived in their district for at least three years," a proposal that may require approval from Congress. Bessent reiterated a recent claim that three Fed presidents do not meet his standards.

Under the current selection process, regional Fed presidents are picked by their bank's board and then must be approved by the Fed's board of governors in Washington. They serve five-year terms, which can be renewed multiple times.

In a CNBC interview last week, Bessent said the Fed needs to streamline its operations, arguing that its approach to overseeing money markets has grown more complex than it needs to be.

data.title: Bayer releases data for contrast agent gadoquatrane in children
data.description:
• Bayer (OTCPK:BAYZF)(OTCPK:BAYRY) released data from a study of low-dose gadoquatrane, an MRI gadolinium-based contrast agent, for use in children.
  • The QUANTI Pediatric trial examined a gadolinium dose of 0.04 mmol Gd/kg body weight, a dose reduction of 60% compared to macrocyclic contrast agents typically dosed at 0.1 mmol Gd/kg body weight.
  • The trial met its primary endpoint, as pharmacokinetic behavior of the agent observed in children was similar to that seen in adults.

data.title: Trump’s robotics push shines spotlight on Cathie Wood’s ARKQ ETF
data.description:
The Trump administration is intensifying its focus on robotics as part of a broader effort to accelerate advancements in artificial intelligence, according to a report published Wednesday.

The renewed push has drawn market attention to robotics-oriented stocks and exchange-traded funds, including Cathie Wood’s ARK Autonomous Technology & Robotics ETF (ARKQ), a prominent vehicle for innovation-driven investors.

With $1.55B in assets under management and 38 portfolio holdings, ARKQ has seen heightened interest as the robotics sector moves further into the spotlight. 

In response to the growing attention, Seeking Alpha has highlighted ARKQ’s top holdings according to its Quant Ratings system, offering investors a data-driven look at the exchange traded fund. 

No. 1: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company (TSM), Quant Rating of 4.97.

No. 2: Advanced Micro Devices (AMD), Quant Rating of 4.79. 

No. 3: L3Harris Technologies (LHX), Quant Rating of 4.40. 

No. 4:  Alphabet (GOOG), Quant Rating of 3.49.

No. 5: Nvidia (NVDA), Quant Rating of 3.49. 

No. 6: Palantir Technologies (PLTR), Quant Rating of 3.48.

No. 7: Amazon (AMZN), Quant Rating of 3.48. 

No. 8: Teradyne (TER), Quant Rating of 3.45. 

No. 9: Symbotic (SYM), Quant Rating of 3.43. 

No. 10: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG), Quant Rating of 3.40.

For more on the robotics and AI space see below a separate group of exchange traded funds that offer access to the space through a diversified vantagepoint:

Artificial Intelligence/Robotics ETFs: (AIQ), (BOTZ), (DTEC), (WTAI), (XAIX), (WISE), (GINN), (ROBT), (TECB), (XT), (THNQ), and (CHAT).
